dismissalText.commentary: Aiden Markram c Washington Sundar b Prasidh Krishna 30 (21b 5x4 1x6 38m) SR: 142.85

dismissalComment.html: 4, 4, W! Markram falls and LSG have lost their top three. Back of a length ball outside off stump, Markram pulls but doesn't get it near the middle. The short arm jab goes only as far as Washy, who is stationed right at cow corner

dismissalText.commentary: Mitchell Marsh c Shubman Gill b Rabada 11 (4b 1x4 1x6 7m) SR: 275

dismissalComment.html: 6, 4, out. What an eventful start to Rabada's spell. But this wicket is all that he will care about. He goes fuller on off stump at 129kph, Marsh looks to go up and over mid-off, but doesn't get it anywhere near the middle of the bat. Hits it straight down mid-off's throat where Gill makes no mistake

dismissalText.commentary: Rishabh Pant c Tewatia b Mohammed Siraj 18 (11b 2x4 1x6 13m) SR: 163.63

dismissalComment.html: Siraj wins the battle against Pant and this is a body blow for LSG. Wobble-seam length ball angling across Pant at 139.9kph. He was looking to smash that across the line again, but the angle causes the miscue as the ball balloons straight up. Tewatia moves to his right from mid-off, settles under it and takes a good catch.

dismissalText.commentary: Ayush Badoni c Phillips b Prasidh Krishna 9 (11b 1x4 0x6 22m) SR: 81.81

dismissalComment.html: Another one bites the dust but GP almost made a mess of that catch. Hard length on off stump, he pulls this but doesn't get any power or timing behind it. The ball goes straight to Phillips at deep midwicket, where he loses it in the sun initially and goes the wrong way. But in the nick of time, he recovers and hangs on

dismissalText.long: c Shubman Gill b Prasidh Krishna
dismissalText.commentary: Nicholas Pooran c Shubman Gill b Prasidh Krishna 19 (21b 0x4 2x6 30m) SR: 90.47

dismissalComment.html: Well, that's another tame end to Pooran's innings! Prasidh continues his wicket-taking ways, and this time it'sthe slower ball that does the job. Dug in short with pace off, and angled across Pooran, who looks to swat it away like a tennis forehand, but ends up lobbing an easy chance to mid-off
